title: Bluff: Geography Surprises
description: One answer is fake — can you spot the bluff?

questions.question: What is the driest place on Earth?
questions.explanation: The McMurdo Dry Valleys in Antarctica have had no rain for nearly 2 million years.

questions.question: Which country has the most islands?
questions.explanation: Sweden has over 267,000 islands, more than any other country.

questions.question: What is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile stretches approximately 6,650 km through northeastern Africa.

questions.question: Which city is on two continents?
questions.explanation: Istanbul straddles Europe and Asia across the Bosphorus strait.

questions.question: What is the smallest country in the world?
questions.explanation: Vatican City is only 0.44 km² — about the size of a golf course.

questions.question: Which lake contains the most water by volume?
questions.explanation: Lake Baikal holds about 20% of the world's unfrozen fresh surface water.

questions.question: What is the highest waterfall in the world?
questions.explanation: Angel Falls in Venezuela drops 979 meters — the world's highest uninterrupted waterfall.

questions.question: Which desert is the largest in the world?
questions.explanation: Antarctica is technically the world's largest desert at 14 million km².

questions.question: Which country has the most time zones?
questions.explanation: France has 12 time zones due to its overseas territories.

questions.question: What is the deepest point in the ocean?
questions.explanation: The Challenger Deep in the Mariana Trench reaches about 10,935 meters.

questions.question: Which African country has the most pyramids?
questions.explanation: Sudan has over 200 pyramids, more than double Egypt's count.

questions.question: What is the most visited country in the world?
questions.explanation: France receives about 90 million international tourists per year.

questions.question: Which country is both in Europe and South America?
questions.explanation: French Guiana is an overseas department of France located in South America.

questions.question: What is the most earthquake-prone country?
questions.explanation: Indonesia sits on the Ring of Fire and experiences the most earthquakes annually.

questions.question: Which capital city is the highest above sea level?
questions.explanation: La Paz, Bolivia sits at about 3,640 meters above sea level.
